message:
chore(beads): epic closeout for 1mfw3 FrankenTUI UX revelation pass (1mfw3.6.6)

## Completion Summary by Workstream

### .1 Command Palette Migration (7 tasks, all closed)
- Migrated custom palette overlay to ftui CommandPalette widget
- 27 PaletteActions with group taxonomy and target dispatch
- Evidence ledger panel with Bayesian score inspection
- Match-type filters (All/Exact/Prefix/WordStart/Substring/Fuzzy)
- Query latency instrumentation with micro-bench toggle
- 124 palette tests, full regression lock-in

### .2 BOCPD Resize Coalescer (7 tasks, all closed)
- Unified ProgramConfig across standard/record/playback paths
- BOCPD resize coalescing with decision evidence capture
- Resize regime summary and evidence rendered in inspector
- Deterministic resize storm tests with evidence integrity
- Macro/inline/fullscreen parity verified

### .3 Explainability Cockpit (7 tasks, all closed)
- 4-panel cockpit taxonomy: DiffStrategy, ResizeRegime, Budget, Timeline
- CockpitMode (Overlay/Expanded) with responsive CockpitTopology
- InspectorTab expansion for diff/budget/timeline surfaces
- Timeline event feed with severity classification
- 34 cockpit tests, no-panic guarantees

### .4 Structural Responsive Layouts (7 tasks, all closed)
- LayoutBreakpoint system: Narrow/MediumNarrow/Medium/Wide
- SearchTopology + AnalyticsTopology per breakpoint
- VisibilityPolicy for controls, hints, split affordances
- Ultra-narrow terminal fallback (<30w or <6h)
- Hit-region and focus invariant reconciliation
- 16-entry SIZE_MATRIX regression suite

### .5 Input Smoothness Pass (1 task, pre-completed)
- Drag jitter filter + hover stabilization UX

### .6 Program Hardening (6 tasks, all closed)
- Cross-workstream integration checklist (19 scenarios)
- Quality gate sweep: clippy/fmt/check all clean
- Performance/regression envelope checks passed
- UBS scan: 0 critical, 0 high findings; 4 dead constants removed
- Operator-facing docs and rationale committed
- This closeout document

## Quality Gates (Final)
- cargo fmt --check: CLEAN
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: CLEAN
- cargo check --all-targets: CLEAN
- cargo test --lib: 2985 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored
- 26 epic commits on main

## Deferred Items
None. All planned workstreams (.1-.6) fully delivered.
No follow-up beads created — the epic scope is complete.

## Known Risks
- app.rs is 22,484 lines — consider splitting in future work
- Concurrent multi-agent editing causes race conditions on app.rs
- 4 style token constants were removed as dead code; if future code
  needs them, re-add with stylesheet definitions

## Handoff Notes
- ftui is the sole TUI runtime (ratatui fully removed in 2noh9.6.1)
- tui.rs must remain a 4-line stub
- CockpitTopology controls inspector sizing per breakpoint
- LayoutBreakpoint drives all responsive behavior
- PaletteMatchMode lives in palette.rs (not app.rs)
- StyleContext methods (agent_accent_style, score_style) in style_system.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Review all tests/*.rs files
- Identify tests not using E2eLogger
- Add structured logging to each test

## Key Tests to Audit
- pages_master_e2e.rs (already uses logger)
- e2e_cli_flows.rs
- e2e_filters.rs
- connector_*.rs tests
- search_pipeline.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All E2E tests emit JSONL logs
- [ ] Test names and suites correctly tagged
- [ ] File and line info included
- [ ] Duration captured for all tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:22:13.259657737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:21:58.293015475Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:21:58.292941518Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: Added E2eLogger infrastructure to e2e_cli_flows.rs as reference implementation. Found 111+ tests across 8 E2E files needing conversion. Created run_logged_test helper for incremental adoption.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lcw","depends_on_id":"30qc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lwxf","title":"Phase 4K: Clipboard integration via OSC52","description":"Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration that works over SSH and in any modern terminal. Add clipboard operations: (1) Copy selected result path+line to clipboard (Ctrl+Y or y), (2) Copy detail pane content to clipboard (Ctrl+C when in detail view without selection), (3) Copy code block from detail view (click or keybinding on specific code block), (4) Copy search query to clipboard, (5) Paste from clipboard into search bar (Ctrl+V -- currently handled by crossterm Paste event, but ftui clipboard provides a unified API). OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without any external dependency -- it works over SSH sessions which is critical for cass multi-machine search. The clipboard module handles terminal capability detection and graceful fallback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:21:52.504734512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027618302Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027591773Z","close_reason":"Content preserved in new 2noh9.3 bead: Clipboard OSC52 integration. Copy paths, content, code blocks; works over SSH merged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lwxf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1p0wb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:45.733329156Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mag","title":"Replace e2e_install_easy fake binaries with real install flow","description":"Eliminate fake rustc/cargo/sha256sum stubs in tests/e2e_install_easy.rs.\n\nDetails:\n- Run install.sh against a real rustup toolchain in a temp HOME (CI + local opt-in).\n- Capture full install logs and verify binary checksums.\n- Keep a fast-path smoke test if needed, but remove fake-bin allowlist entry.","acceptance_criteria":"1) e2e_install_easy runs install.sh with real rustup/cargo in temp HOME.
2) Logs capture stdout/stderr and checksum verification.
3) Fake-bin allowlist entry removed (unless documented exception).
4) Test can be gated by env for local runs; always runs in CI.","notes":"Notes:
- Prefer a dedicated CI job for real install flow.
- Ensure cleanup does not delete user data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:13:23.114573078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:06.963139681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:06.963000452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mag","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ul61","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","title":"FrankenTUI UX revelation pass for cass TUI","description":"FrankenTUI UX Revelation Program for cass.

Why this exists:
The current cass TUI has strong fundamentals (virtualized rendering, adaptive degradation, robust tests), but still under-leverages FrankenTUI's differentiators: explainable runtime decisions, evidence-driven interaction UX, and regime-aware responsiveness under input/resize stress.

Strategic intent:
1. Make the UI feel materially more responsive under real-world terminal conditions.
2. Make adaptive behavior debuggable and explainable in-product.
3. Raise interaction quality so command, resize, and pointer behavior are stable and predictable.
4. Keep implementation decisions auditable via tests and explicit acceptance criteria.

Program structure:
- .1 Command palette migration to evidence-driven FrankenTUI patterns.
- .2 BOCPD resize coalescer + decision evidence surfaced in cass UI.
- .3 Explainability cockpit overlay/paneling (diff/resize/budget/timeline).
- .4 Structural responsive layouts by breakpoint topology.
- .5 Input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization) [already completed].
- .6 Program-level hardening + release gates.

Dependency policy:
- Runtime evidence plumbing (.2) must precede full cockpit timeline capabilities (.3).
- Palette migration (.1) and responsive layouts (.4) can progress in parallel.
- Hardening/release gate (.6) blocks final close of this epic.

Success definition:
- Core workstreams .1 .2 .3 .4 implemented with green quality gates.
- .6 hardening confirms no regressions in stability/perf/usability.
- New behavior is self-explanatory through inspector/cockpit signals.
- Beads are sufficient for future contributors without external planning docs.","design":"Architecture principle: observable adaptivity.

Every adaptive decision (diff/resize/budget/interaction) should be represented as:
1) deterministic runtime behavior,
2) evidence that can be surfaced in UI,
3) tests that lock in expected behavior.

This avoids black-box UX drift and enables root-cause debugging directly from cass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:24.570821358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:33.418607016Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program note: prioritize functional responsiveness and explainability over visual novelty. If a proposed UI change looks flashy but does not improve operator speed, stability, or debuggability, it is out of scope for this epic. The highest leverage is in making adaptive behavior legible and trustworthy.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","title":"Migrate cass command palette to FrankenTUI evidence-driven palette","description":"Workstream .1: Migrate cass command palette to FrankenTUI evidence-driven palette.

Background:
cass currently uses a local PaletteState + custom overlay renderer. This delivers basic filtering/navigation but lacks explainability, rich match-mode controls, and built-in search quality telemetry. FrankenTUI already ships command-palette primitives with Bayesian evidence and match-type filters.

Goals:
1) Replace custom overlay behavior with ftui command_palette capabilities where practical.
2) Preserve cass action semantics and shortcut discoverability.
3) Expose match evidence and query-latency signals so users can trust ranking output.
4) Maintain deterministic keyboard-first operation and low-latency feel.

In scope:
- Action model mapping from current PaletteAction catalog.
- Rendering + interaction migration in app overlay path.
- Match filter controls and evidence ledger UI.
- Regression tests for palette behavior.

Out of scope:
- Unrelated command renaming churn.
- New non-cass commands not tied to current operator workflows.

Validation expectations:
- Existing palette tests continue to pass or are replaced with stronger equivalents.
- Manual checks confirm no input lag and no focus-trap regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.263824178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T03:32:33.032790473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T03:32:33.032760507Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.263824178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: keep the migration adapter-centric. Avoid scattering palette-specific branching throughout unrelated app update/render paths. Preserve all existing action semantics first, then add evidence/filter enhancements in incremental steps to minimize regression surface.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","title":"Palette migration discovery and action mapping contract","description":"Subtask .1.1

Objective:
Create an explicit mapping between current cass palette semantics and FrankenTUI command_palette concepts so migration is deliberate rather than ad hoc.

Deliverables:
- Action inventory with grouping (theme/filter/saved-view/analytics/screenshot/macro/sources).
- Mapping table to target action identifiers and display metadata.
- Interaction contract for keyboard navigation, focus trap behavior, and close/execute semantics.

Rationale:
A clear mapping contract de-risks migration by preventing accidental command loss, hint regressions, or semantic drift.

Done when:
All current palette actions are mapped and no action is left without an explicit target behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:51:17.807563772Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T00:51:17.807541641Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","title":"Implement palette adapter layer for cass action dispatch","description":"Subtask .1.2

Objective:
Introduce a focused adapter translating FrankenTUI palette selection events into existing cass message/action dispatch, preserving business semantics.

Deliverables:
- Adapter structs/functions that bridge palette result -> CassMsg or equivalent action path.
- Compatibility handling for slot-based actions and existing shortcuts.
- Clear extension points for future palette actions.

Rationale:
A dedicated adapter isolates migration complexity and prevents palette widget internals from leaking into broad app logic.

Done when:
Palette action execution parity is maintained with minimal changes to downstream command handlers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:57.119725562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:07:51.705996907Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:07:51.705974676Z","close_reason":"Adapter layer complete: PaletteResult enum, dispatch(), execute_selected(), palette_result_to_cmd() + 28 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:57.119725562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.413671666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","title":"Refactor analytics surface responsive structure and tab/header behavior","description":"Objective:
Apply structured responsive layout principles to analytics surface header/content/footer composition.

Deliverables:
- Breakpoint-aware analytics header/tab layout adjustments.
- Content region structural behavior for constrained widths.
- Footer/status behavior consistency with search surface conventions.

Why this matters:
Analytics surface should remain equally usable and coherent across terminal sizes.

Done when:
Analytics layout adapts predictably with no brittle breakpoint-specific hacks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.859098174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:34:37.838389051Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:34:37.838365517Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.859098174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.756287998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","title":"Responsive layout regression suite across size matrix and interaction invariants","description":"Objective:
Create comprehensive tests that validate responsive behavior across representative terminal sizes and interaction pathways.

Deliverables:
- Unit tests for topology selection, pane-visibility policy, and hit-region/focus invariants.
- E2e size-sweep scripts (small/medium/wide/very large, including ultra-narrow fallback) with keyboard and mouse interaction flows.
- Structured logging artifacts for each scenario (resolved topology, visible panes, focus transitions, hit-target map, assertion outcomes, timings).
- Diffable regression summaries highlighting behavior changes by breakpoint.

Why this matters:
Responsive logic is high-risk for subtle regressions; broad, deterministic unit+e2e coverage with logs is essential for trust.

Done when:
Responsive behavior is locked in by comprehensive automated coverage and actionable diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:15.068136009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:33:45.163886099Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T00:33:45.163861744Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:15.068136009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.414365619Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.664953474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.915432798Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.248432103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.482471786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.5","title":"Add input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization UX)","description":"Workstream .5: Input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization UX).

Status note:
This workstream is marked closed with reported delivery of drag jitter filtering + hover stabilization tests. It remains documented here as a completed pillar of the overall UX revelation program.

Completed intent:
- Reduce pointer jitter and drag noise.
- Improve predictability of hover/selection interactions.
- Add focused tests to lock in behavior.

Program dependency context:
Downstream work should assume this baseline exists and build on it rather than re-solving the same interaction stability layer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.952817550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:54:58.624889833Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T22:49:00.071932518Z","close_reason":"Completed: drag jitter filter + hover stabilization, 6 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.952817550Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","title":"Program hardening and release gates for FrankenTUI UX overhaul","description":"Workstream .6: program-level hardening, integration validation, and release closure for the FrankenTUI UX overhaul.

Purpose:
Even strong feature work can regress UX quality if cross-workstream interactions are not validated holistically. This workstream ensures the combined system is stable, fast, and operationally understandable before epic closure.

In scope:
- Cross-workstream integration checks.
- Quality gate execution and bug-fix loop.
- Performance and reliability regression detection.
- Comprehensive unit-test and e2e script execution with structured logging artifacts for auditability.
- Final operator-facing documentation and closeout criteria.

Out of scope:
- Net-new feature expansion unrelated to closure quality.

Success signal:
All prerequisite workstreams are integrated, quality gates are green, test/e2e logs are complete, and closure notes provide clear handoff context for future contributors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:05:32.778845681Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closeout guidance: do not close this workstream based solely on passing unit tests. Require integrated manual scenarios and performance sanity checks. Any unresolved UX ambiguity should become explicit follow-up beads before epic closure.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:36Z"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","title":"Program hardening and release gates for FrankenTUI UX overhaul","description":"Workstream .6: program-level hardening, integration validation, and release closure for the FrankenTUI UX overhaul.

Purpose:
Even strong feature work can regress UX quality if cross-workstream interactions are not validated holistically. This workstream ensures the combined system is stable, fast, and operationally understandable before epic closure.

In scope:
- Cross-workstream integration checks.
- Quality gate execution and bug-fix loop.
- Performance and reliability regression detection.
- Comprehensive unit-test and e2e script execution with structured logging artifacts for auditability.
- Final operator-facing documentation and closeout criteria.

Out of scope:
- Net-new feature expansion unrelated to closure quality.

Success signal:
All prerequisite workstreams are integrated, quality gates are green, test/e2e logs are complete, and closure notes provide clear handoff context for future contributors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T04:05:39.196548083Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T04:05:39.196524869Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closeout guidance: do not close this workstream based solely on passing unit tests. Require integrated manual scenarios and performance sanity checks. Any unresolved UX ambiguity should become explicit follow-up beads before epic closure.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","title":"Assemble cross-workstream integration checklist and execution plan","description":"Objective:
Create one integration checklist early that validates interactions among palette, resize evidence, cockpit, and responsive layout changes, and translates them into executable test runs.

Deliverables:
- Cross-workstream scenario matrix (normal, stress, edge) with explicit expected outcomes.
- Mapping from scenarios to required unit suites and e2e scripts across .1/.2/.3/.4 workstreams.
- Canonical structured-log schema for integration/e2e runs (scenario_id, mode, event stream, timing, assertion outcomes).
- Triage playbook for first-failure diagnosis and ownership of each unresolved gap.

Why this matters:
Cross-feature interactions are where subtle regressions hide; defining execution and logging contracts up front prevents late-stage ambiguity.

Done when:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","title":"P2.2 Indexer multi-root orchestration","description":"# Indexer Multi-Root Orchestration

## Context
The indexer needs to build a list of scan roots and orchestrate scanning across them, injecting provenance appropriately.

## Location
src/indexer/mod.rs

## Current Flow
1. Build single ScanContext with data_dir
2. For each connector, call scan()
3. Persist results

## New Flow
1. Build list of scan roots:
   - Local roots (from watch_roots() or equivalent)
   - Remote mirror roots (from sources table)
2. Group roots by connector affinity
3. For each connector:
   - For each relevant root:
     - Create ScanContext with that root
     - Call scan()
     - Inject provenance into results
     - Persist with source_id

## Implementation

### Build Scan Roots List
\`\`\`rust
fn build_scan_roots(storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Vec<ScanRoot> {
    let mut roots = Vec::new();
    
    // Add local roots (existing logic from watch_roots())
    for local_root in local_default_roots() {
        roots.push(ScanRoot {
            path: local_root,
            origin: Origin::local(),
            platform: Some(Platform::current()),
            workspace_rewrites: vec![],
        });
    }
    
    // Add remote mirror roots
    for source in storage.list_sources()? {
        if source.kind == SourceKind::Ssh {
            let mirror_root = data_dir.join("remotes").join(&source.id).join("mirror");
            if mirror_root.exists() {
                roots.push(ScanRoot {
                    path: mirror_root,
                    origin: Origin {
                        source_id: source.id.clone(),
                        kind: source.kind,
                        host: source.host_label.clone(),
                    },
                    platform: source.platform,
                    workspace_rewrites: parse_rewrites(&source.config),
                });
            }
        }
    }
    
    roots
}
\`\`\`

### Connector Affinity
Not all roots apply to all connectors. For example:
- ClaudeCodeConnector cares about ~/.claude or remotes/*/mirror/**/.claude
- AiderConnector cares about .aider.chat.history.md anywhere

For now: let connectors handle root relevance via their detect() logic.

### Provenance Injection
After connector.scan() returns:
\`\`\`rust
for conv in conversations.iter_mut() {
    conv.metadata["cass"] = json!({
        "origin": {
            "source_id": scan_root.origin.source_id,
            "kind": scan_root.origin.kind.as_str(),
            "host": scan_root.origin.host,
        }
    });
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- P2.1 (ScanContext extended)
- P1.3 (storage accepts source_id)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Indexer builds list of scan roots
- [ ] Local roots work as before
- [ ] Provenance injected into metadata
- [ ] Results persisted with correct source_id
- [ ] Tests verify multi-root behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:57:31.805169Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","close_reason":"Implemented multi-root orchestration with build_scan_roots(), inject_provenance(), and provenance extraction in persist::map_to_internal(). All 294 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","title":"Task 4: Update template.rs and lib.rs integration","description":"# Objective
Update the HtmlExporter and lib.rs export flow to use the new MessageGroup system.

## Changes Required

### 1. HtmlExporter::export_messages signature update
In src/html_export/template.rs:

```rust
pub fn export_messages(
    &self,
    title: &str,
    groups: &[MessageGroup],  // Changed from &[Message]
    metadata: TemplateMetadata,
    password: Option<&str>,
) -> Result<String, TemplateError>
```

### 2. lib.rs run_export_html integration
Around line 10302, after creating Vec<Message>:

```rust
// OLD:
let messages: Vec<Message> = raw_messages.iter()...collect();

// NEW:
let flat_messages: Vec<Message> = raw_messages.iter()...collect();
let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages);

// Update metadata message count
let metadata = TemplateMetadata {
    message_count: message_groups.len(), // or sum of all messages
    ...
};

// Pass groups to exporter
let html = exporter.export_messages(title, &message_groups, metadata, final_password.as_deref())?;
```

### 3. Update RenderOptions usage
Ensure render options properly flow through to grouped rendering:
- show_tool_calls flag should control whether badges are shown
- show_timestamps applies to group primary message
- collapse_threshold applies to group primary content

### 4. Backwards Compatibility
We do NOT care about backwards compatibility (per AGENTS.md). Change the API directly, update all call sites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] export_messages accepts MessageGroup slice
- [ ] lib.rs calls grouping function before export
- [ ] Metadata reflects correct counts
- [ ] All compile errors resolved
- [ ] Integration works end-to-end","notes":"## Metadata Count Clarification

The TemplateMetadata.message_count field should reflect:
- **Total messages** (sum of all messages in all groups), NOT group count
- This maintains backwards compatibility with existing exports
- Add new field if needed: group_count

```rust
let metadata = TemplateMetadata {
    message_count: message_groups.iter()
        .map(|g| 1 + g.tool_calls.len()) // primary + tools
        .sum(),
    // Or add: group_count: message_groups.len(),
    ...
};
```

## CLI Flag Behavior

### --include-tools flag
- TRUE (default): Show tool badges in message headers
- FALSE: Hide all tool badges, show only message content

### --show-timestamps flag
- Applies to group primary message timestamp
- Tool individual timestamps not shown (too noisy)

### --no-cdns flag
- Unchanged behavior
- All CSS/JS still inlined when CDNs disabled

### --theme flag
- Unchanged: "dark" or "light" default
- Applied to grouped messages same as before

## OpenCode Session Handling

The existing `load_opencode_session_for_export()` returns raw messages.
These still need to go through the grouping function:

```rust
if is_opencode {
    match load_opencode_session_for_export(session_path) {
        Ok((title, start, end, msgs)) => {
            // msgs are raw serde_json::Value, convert to Message first
            let flat_messages = msgs.iter()
                .filter_map(|m| convert_opencode_message(m))
                .collect();
            let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages, AgentFormat::OpenCode);
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

If grouping fails (shouldn't, but for safety):
```rust
let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages, agent_format);
if message_groups.is_empty() && !flat_messages.is_empty() {
    warn!(
        flat_count = flat_messages.len(),
        "Grouping produced no groups from non-empty messages, falling back"
    );
    // Fallback: each message becomes its own group
    message_groups = flat_messages.into_iter()
        .map(|m| MessageGroup::new(m, MessageGroupType::from_role(&m.role)))
        .collect();
}
